slides.title: A Shared Reality ☁️
slides.content:
We learned that Objects are independent. They each get their own copy of Attributes (like `name`).

But what if we wanted a variable that is **shared** across ALL dogs? For example, a `totalDogCount` integer that goes up every time ANY dog is born.

We use the **`static`** keyword.

slides.title: Static Attributes ☁️
slides.content:
Declaring an attribute as `static` attaches it to the **Class Blueprint** itself, rather than the Object Instances.

Every time you `new Dog()`, it increments the shared `totalCount`.

quizData.question: How do you correctly access a `static` variable?

options.text: You can't access it outside the class.

quizData.explanation: Because static variables belong to the Blueprint, you query the Class Name directly (e.g., Math.PI is a static variable inside the Math class!)

slides.title: Static Methods 🛠️
slides.content:
Methods can be static too. 

If a method is `static`, you can run it WITHOUT bothering to construct an object first.

Have you noticed our `public static void main` method? Java runs `main` instantly when the program starts, without ever creating a `new Main()` object. That's why it's static!
